title: LoopCliConfig
description: Configuration for a loop-the-loop run. Matches the LoopCliConfig type defined in src/types.ts.

$schema.description: Optional URI to this JSON Schema, used by editors for validation.

name.description: Task name. Used for report filenames and git commit messages.

outputDir.description: Directory for report and state files. Defaults to the config file directory when loaded via the CLI. Relative paths are resolved against the config file directory.

maxPrompts.description: Stop after processing this many prompts. Unlimited when omitted.

maxBudgetUsd.description: Lifetime USD budget across resumes. The loop stops after the prompt whose completion takes the persisted total at or above this cap, and stops immediately at startup if it is already at or above it. Track-only when omitted.

concurrency.description: Number of prompts to run concurrently in one process. Defaults to 1 (serial). Not supported with allowSourceUpdate or the batch prompt generator.

interPromptPause.description: Seconds to pause between prompts. Helps with rate limits.

allowSourceUpdate.description: When true, require a clean git working tree, allow agents to modify source, and commit after each successful prompt.

logger.description: Controls verbose diagnostic logging to stderr.

modelPrice.description: Per-million-token prices for one model. cacheRead/cacheCreation/reasoning rates default to multiples of input/output when omitted.

agentSpec.description: Which agent to use. Either a bare agent name (for agents that take no init config) or a [agentName, config] tuple.

responses.description: Canned responses returned in order from invoke(). Each entry has a status of 'success', 'glitch', or 'error'.

repeat.description: What to do once the responses list is exhausted. 'none' (default) returns an error on every subsequent invoke; 'cycle' wraps back to the first response and keeps going.

timeoutMs.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for a single Codex invocation before sending SIGTERM (then SIGKILL after a short grace period). When omitted, no timeout is applied.

model.description: Codex model to run (forwarded to `codex exec --model`) and used for cost estimation. Falls back to the CODEX_MODEL env var when omitted.

prices.description: Per-model prices keyed by model id, used to estimate USD cost. When the resolved model is unpriced the agent records tokens only (costSource 'unavailable').

systemPrompt.description: Optional system prompt appended to the built-in workspace guidance. Supports {{include:path}} macros (resolved relative to the config file directory).

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema describing the expected shape of structured output. When provided, the SDK returns structured data conforming to the schema.

model.description: Optional OpenAI model name. When omitted, the Agents SDK default is used.

modelSettings.description: Optional model-specific settings forwarded to the OpenAI Agents SDK.

maxTurns.description: Maximum number of agent turns per prompt invocation.

prices.description: Per-model prices keyed by model id, used to estimate USD cost. When the resolved model is unpriced the agent records tokens only (costSource 'unavailable').

systemPrompt.description: Optional system prompt prepended to the conversation. Supports {{include:path}} macros (resolved relative to the config file directory).

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema describing the expected shape of structured output. When provided, the SDK returns structured data conforming to the schema.

allowedTools.description: Tool names or permission patterns auto-allowed without permission prompts. This is the auto-approval list only and does not restrict which built-in tools are loaded; use `loadedTools` for that. Bare names (e.g. 'Read') and patterns (e.g. 'Bash(gh issue create *)') are both accepted.

loadedTools.description: Controls which built-in tools the SDK loads (the SDK's `tools` option). Provide an array of tool names to restrict the set, `[]` to disable all built-in tools, or `{ type: 'preset', preset: 'claude_code' }` to load the default Claude Code preset. When omitted the SDK falls back to its own default (the `claude_code` preset).

disallowedTools.description: Tool names that are explicitly blocked.

mcpServers.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configurations. Keys are server names, values are server configs.

maxTurns.description: Maximum number of agent turns per prompt invocation.

model.description: Claude model to use. Accepts an alias ('sonnet', 'opus', 'haiku') or a full model id (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7', 'claude-sonnet-4-6'). When omitted the SDK falls back to its CLI default.

fallbackModel.description: Fallback model used when the primary model is unavailable (e.g. rate limited or temporarily down). Forwarded to the SDK's `fallbackModel`.

effort.description: Reasoning effort applied per turn. The SDK silently downgrades unsupported levels per model.

thinking.description: Extended-thinking configuration. `adaptive` lets the model decide (default on Opus 4.6+); `enabled` fixes a token budget; `disabled` turns thinking off.

additionalDirectories.description: Additional absolute directories the agent may read/write. Forwarded unchanged to the SDK's `additionalDirectories`.

promptGeneratorSpec.description: Tuple of [generatorName, config] selecting and configuring a prompt generator.

filePattern.description: Glob pattern for files to process (e.g. 'src/**/*.ts').

promptTemplate.description: Prompt template. {{file}} is replaced with each file path. Supports {{include:path}} macros (resolved relative to the config file directory).

Prompt template. Substituted placeholders: {{id}}, {{summary}}, {{url}}, {{component}}, {{product}}, {{severity}}, {{status}}, {{assignee}}, {{whiteboard}}. Supports {{include:path}} macros (resolved relative to the config file directory).

Prompt template. Substituted placeholders: {{id}}, {{number}}, {{repository}}, {{owner}}, {{repo}}, {{title}}, {{url}}, {{state}}, {{author}}, {{assignee}}, {{assignees}}, {{labels}}, {{milestone}}, {{commentCount}}, {{createdAt}}, {{updatedAt}}, {{closedAt}}, {{body}}. Supports {{include:path}} macros (resolved relative to the config file directory).

origin.description: GitHub REST API origin. Defaults to https://api.github.com. For GitHub Enterprise, use the API origin, for example https://github.example.com/api/v3.

token.description: GitHub bearer token. Prefer tokenEnv for CLI JSON configs.

tokenEnv.description: Environment variable name from which to read the GitHub bearer token. Defaults to GITHUB_TOKEN then GH_TOKEN.

apiVersion.description: GitHub REST API version header. Defaults to 2022-11-28.

userAgent.description: User-Agent header value for GitHub API requests. Defaults to loop-the-loop.

repository.description: Repository to search, in owner/repo form.

query.description: GitHub issue search syntax. The client adds repo:<repository> and is:issue.

sort.description: Sort field accepted by GitHub issue search, such as comments, created, or updated.

perPage.description: Number of results to request per API page. GitHub accepts 1 to 100.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of issues to return across all pages. When omitted, returns up to GitHub's 1,000-result search limit. Larger values are capped at 1,000.

dryRun.description: When true, skip the actual query and return an empty set.

logQuery.description: When true, log the query URL to stdout before sending.

promptTemplate.description: Prompt template. Substituted placeholders: {{id}}, {{iid}}, {{project}}, {{title}}, {{url}}, {{state}}, {{author}}, {{assignee}}, {{assignees}}, {{labels}}, {{milestone}}, {{commentCount}}, {{createdAt}}, {{updatedAt}}, {{closedAt}}, {{description}}. Supports {{include:path}} macros (resolved relative to the config file directory).

origin.description: GitLab REST API origin. Defaults to https://gitlab.com/api/v4. For GitLab Self-Managed, use the API origin, for example https://gitlab.example.com/api/v4.

token.description: GitLab access token. Prefer tokenEnv for CLI JSON configs.

tokenEnv.description: Environment variable name from which to read the GitLab access token. Defaults to GITLAB_TOKEN then GL_TOKEN.

userAgent.description: User-Agent header value for GitLab API requests. Defaults to loop-the-loop.

project.description: Project to search, as a numeric project ID or namespaced project path.

state.description: Return all issues or just opened or closed issues.

labels.description: Labels that all returned issues must have.

search.description: Search project issues against their title and description.

milestone.description: Milestone title. GitLab also accepts None and Any.

authorUsername.description: Return issues created by this username.

assigneeUsername.description: Return issues assigned to this username.

scope.description: Return issues for the given scope.

orderBy.description: Sort field accepted by GitLab project issue search, such as created_at, updated_at, priority, or due_date.

createdAfter.description: Return issues created on or after the given ISO 8601 timestamp.

createdBefore.description: Return issues created on or before the given ISO 8601 timestamp.

updatedAfter.description: Return issues updated on or after the given ISO 8601 timestamp.

updatedBefore.description: Return issues updated on or before the given ISO 8601 timestamp.

issueType.description: Filter to a GitLab issue type, for example issue, incident, task, or test_case.

perPage.description: Number of results to request per API page. GitLab accepts 1 to 100.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of issues to return across all pages.

dryRun.description: When true, skip the actual query and return an empty set.

logQuery.description: When true, log the query URL to stdout before sending.

origin.description: Bugzilla instance origin. Defaults to https://bugzilla.mozilla.org.

dryRun.description: When true, skip the actual query and return an empty set.

logQuery.description: When true, log the query URL to stdout before sending.

product.description: Restrict the search to a single product.

bugFields.description: Restrict the response to a subset of bug fields.

bugzillaChangeClause.description: Detect bugs whose 'field' changed from one value to another within the given window. Dates are parsed as UTC midnight.

field.description: Bugzilla field name to watch for changes (e.g. 'bug_status').

from.description: Start of the change window in yyyy-MM-dd format.

to.description: End of the change window in yyyy-MM-dd format.

value.description: The value the field changed to.
Value to compare against. Strings only; dates use 'yyyy-MM-dd' format.

data.description: Inline JSON value (array or object) to iterate over. Mutually exclusive with dataFile.

dataFile.description: Path to a JSON file to read. Resolved relative to the config file directory. Mutually exclusive with data.

path.description: Dot-notation path into the JSON to reach the array or object to iterate over (e.g. 'results.bugs').

idField.description: Field name on each element to use as the unique tracking ID. Defaults to the array index or object key.

promptTemplate.description: Prompt template. Placeholders for object elements: {{fieldName}}, {{id}}, {{index}}. For non-object elements: {{value}}. Supports {{include:path}} macros (resolved relative to the config file directory).

stateFile.description: Path to a strict v2 loop-state file. Config-relative, or a {{steps.<name>.state}} handoff substitution. A missing file is treated as empty input.

promptTemplate.description: Prompt template. Placeholders: {{id}}, {{status}}, and {{reason}} (error outcomes only). Supports {{include:path}} macros.

select.description: Which terminal outcomes to yield. Defaults to success.

prompts.description: Prompts to yield, in order. Prompt IDs are stable stringified array indices.

source.description: Inner prompt generator. Same shape as the top-level promptGenerator tuple.

batchSize.description: Number of source prompts per batch.

summaryPromptTemplate.description: Template for the summary prompt injected after each batch. Available placeholders: {{batchSize}}, {{batchIds}}, {{reportFile}}. Supports {{include:path}} macros (resolved relative to the config file directory).

reportFile.description: Path to the report file the loop is writing to. Injected as {{reportFile}} in the summary template.
